title: Head of Communications & Corporate Affairs - Executive Tier 1 (ET1)
description: Head of Communications & Corporate Affairs is accountable for work associated with one or more of the following: 'General Communications & Corporate Affairs 'Employee Communications & Collaboration 'Corporate Affairs 'Investor Relations Positions are top corporate executives who determine ultimate direction of the entire multinational/global organization. Establish and implement long-term strategy of the enterprise. Include Head of Organization as well as Head of Major Function roles in organization's top entities (Corporate/Group). May include leaders of the largest business/regional units. Typically lead multiple Tier 2 and Tier 3 executives.

title: Head of Communications & Corporate Affairs - Executive Tier 2 (ET2)
description: Head of Communications & Corporate Affairs is accountable for work associated with one or more of the following: 'General Communications & Corporate Affairs 'Employee Communications & Collaboration 'Corporate Affairs 'Investor Relations Positions are top executives typically responsible for a business unit/regional business function. Participate in the development of corporate strategy with specific input to their functional area and line of business. Include corporate function executives reporting to the executive officers of the entire organization. Tier 2 Head of Organization leads a regional business or a local independent company. Typically oversee multiple Tier 3 executives and other Tier 2 executives within their respective function.

title: Head of Communications & Corporate Affairs - Executive Tier 3 (ET3)
description: Head of Communications & Corporate Affairs is accountable for work associated with one or more of the following: 'General Communications & Corporate Affairs 'Employee Communications & Collaboration 'Corporate Affairs 'Investor Relations Positions are executives leading a major function within local entity/business unit or a part of a regional/global business function. They participate in strategy development as it applies to functional area. Tier 3 Head of Organization usually leads local subsidiaries/divisions. Typically lead directors and managers, but may also lead other Tier 3 executives.

title: Head of Communications & Corporate Affairs - Senior Director (M6)
description: Head of Communications & Corporate Affairs is accountable for work associated with one or more of the following: 'General Communications & Corporate Affairs 'Employee Communications & Collaboration 'Corporate Affairs 'Investor Relations A Senior Director (M6) is responsible for strategy execution and operational direction of a business function within local entity/business unit or at the regional level. They may participate in strategy development for their functional area. Works closely with executive leadership concerning matters of significance to the organization. Typically manages multiple teams led by directors and senior managers.

title: General Communications & Corporate Affairs - Executive Tier 2 (ET2)
description: General Communications & Corporate Affairs includes work managed or performed across multiple Communications & Corporate Affairs sub-families. Specializations in this sub-family typically perform work related to both internal and external communications, including developing the content for and producing written and visual communications. The internal portion of the work is focused on company-wide communications to employees related to organization values/strategy/performance and employee programs, policies, and tools. The external communications portion of the work includes aspects of one or more of the following: 'Public Relations 'Government Relations 'Community Relations/Corporate Responsibility 'Investor Relations In some organizations, incumbents may also develop materials for marketing/advertising communications. Positions are top executives typically responsible for a business unit/regional business function. Participate in the development of corporate strategy with specific input to their functional area and line of business. Include corporate function executives reporting to the executive officers of the entire organization. Tier 2 Head of Organization leads a regional business or a local independent company. Typically oversee multiple Tier 3 executives and other Tier 2 executives within their respective function.

title: Head of Customer Service - Executive Tier 1 (ET1)
description: Head of Customer Service is accountable for work associated with one or more of the following: 'General Customer Service 'Customer Relationship Management, Issue Resolution & Account Activation 'Contact Center Operations & Training Positions are top corporate executives who determine ultimate direction of the entire multinational/global organization. Establish and implement long-term strategy of the enterprise. Include Head of Organization as well as Head of Major Function roles in organization's top entities (Corporate/Group). May include leaders of the largest business/regional units. Typically lead multiple Tier 2 and Tier 3 executives.

title: General Customer Service - Team Leader (Para-Professionals) (M1)
description: General Customer Service includes post-sale technical and/or non-technical customer service and support across multiple sub-families for business and/or end-consumer customers including: Remote Customer Service: Providing customer service and support via phone, online chat, or text including: 'Call center-based customer support in response to a high volume of low complexity inquiries 'Customer issues analysis and resolution (typically performed in an office environment) in response to a lower volume of higher complexity inquiries Distribution Center Customer Service: Performed in a distribution center, product returns/repair center, or field walk-in customer service facility including: 'Acting as liaison between customers, production and distribution departments related to specific customer orders 'Providing technical and non-technical customer support in a walk-in service center Incumbents matching to this specialization are not compensated based on achievement of sales targets. title: Customer Experience Strategy - Executive Tier 2 (ET2)
description: Responsible for the strategy, design, engagement and implementation of the organization's brand experience including its products and services. Establishes the overarching vision and defines organization-wide strategic imperatives by transforming the brand experience for customers, vendors and team members. Positions are top executives typically responsible for a business unit/regional business function. title: Head of Sales & Marketing - Executive Tier 1 (ET1)
description: Head of Sales & Marketing is accountable for sales and marketing strategy, teams/processes, and achieving sales revenue and product management profit and loss targets including: 'Direct and Indirect Field Sales, Account Management, and Remote/Telesales 'Sales Operations & Administration 'Product and Brand Marketing 'Product Management 'Advertising & Marketing Communications 'Market Research & Analysis Positions are top corporate executives who determine ultimate direction of the entire multinational/global organization. Establish and implement long-term strategy of the enterprise. Include Head of Organization as well as Head of Major Function roles in organization's top entities (Corporate/Group). May include leaders of the largest business/regional units. Typically lead multiple Tier 2 and Tier 3 executives.

Typically oversee multiple Tier 3 executives and other Tier 2 executives within their respective function.

Establish and implement long-term strategy of the enterprise. Include Head of Organization as well as Head of Major Function roles in organization's top entities (Corporate/Group). May include leaders of the largest business/regional units. Typically lead multiple Tier 2 and Tier 3 executives.

title: Head of Marketing - Executive Tier 2 (ET2)
description: Head of Marketing is accountable for marketing/product management strategy, marketing teams/processes, and achieving product management profit and loss targets including: 'Product and Brand Marketing 'Product Management 'Direct/Telemarketing 'Channel/Trade Marketing 'Digital/Internet Marketing 'Advertising & Marketing Communications 'Market Research & Analysis Positions are top executives typically responsible for a business unit/regional business function. Participate in the development of corporate strategy with specific input to their functional area and line of business. Include corporate function executives reporting to the executive officers of the entire organization. Tier 2 Head of Organization leads a regional business or a local independent company. Typically oversee multiple Tier 3 executives and other Tier 2 executives within their respective function.

title: Head of Marketing - Executive Tier 3 (ET3)
description: Head of Marketing is accountable for marketing/product management strategy, marketing teams/processes, and achieving product management profit and loss targets including: 'Product and Brand Marketing 'Product Management 'Direct/Telemarketing 'Channel/Trade Marketing 'Digital/Internet Marketing 'Advertising & Marketing Communications 'Market Research & Analysis Positions are executives leading a major function within local entity/business unit or a part of a regional/global business function. They participate in strategy development as it applies to functional area. Tier 3 Head of Organization usually leads local subsidiaries/divisions. Typically lead directors and managers, but may also lead other Tier 3 executives.

title: Head of Marketing - Senior Director (M6)
description: Head of Marketing is accountable for marketing/product management strategy, marketing teams/processes, and achieving product management profit and loss targets including: 'Product and Brand Marketing 'Product Management 'Direct/Telemarketing 'Channel/Trade Marketing 'Digital/Internet Marketing 'Advertising & Marketing Communications 'Market Research & Analysis A Senior Director (M6) is responsible for strategy execution and operational direction of a business function within local entity/business unit or at the regional level. They may participate in strategy development for their functional area. Works closely with executive leadership concerning matters of significance to the organization. Typically manages multiple teams led by directors and senior managers.

title: General Sales & Marketing - Executive Tier 2 (ET2)
description: General Sales & Marketing work focuses on a combination of general sales and marketing work including: 'Face-to-face and/or remote sales to new or existing customers 'Assessing customer needs and suggesting appropriate products, services and/or solutions 'Planning, developing, and delivering the marketing strategy for products/services and associated brand(s) 'Evaluating, measuring, and managing the product/brand P&L (e.g., budgeting, expenditures, profitability, return-on-investment, etc.) Incumbents in this specialization may also provide customer service and support in the form of information on product/price and resolution to issues related to billing, shipping, delivery, complaints, etc. Incumbents matching to this specialization are compensated based on achievement of sales targets. Positions are top executives typically responsible for a business unit/regional business function. Participate in the development of corporate strategy with specific input to their functional area and line of business. Include corporate function executives reporting to the executive officers of the entire organization. Tier 2 Head of Organization leads a regional business or a local independent company. Typically oversee multiple Tier 3 executives and other Tier 2 executives within their respective function.

Develops capture strategy including business solution for pursuit of opportunities; identifies potential customers, competitive assessments, teaming suppliers, and prices-to-win; participates in bid and proposal activities. Maintains on-going interaction with marketing. Positions are executives leading a major function within local entity/business unit or a part of a regional/global business function. They participate in strategy development as it applies to functional area. Tier 3 Head of Organization usually leads local subsidiaries/divisions. Typically lead directors and managers, but may also lead other Tier 3 executives.

title: Capture Management - Team Leader (Professionals) (M2)
description: Leads capture efforts to secure new or re-compete contracts, or task order business. Maintains on-going interaction with marketing. A Team Leader (M2) supervises professional level employees (typically entry or experienced level) and may also supervise some para-professional employees. description: Designs and maintains reward programs to drive customer brand loyalty, with particular focus on identifying and retaining the best customers as well as attracting new customers. title: Product Management (Automotive, High Tech, Life Sciences) - Executive Tier 3 (ET3)
description: Responsible for managing the entire product life cycle for certain product(s) (e.g., hardware, software, wireless, web products, medical devices, etc.) from planning to execution in support of organizational strategy and objectives. Researches and analyzes market conditions, and identifies key consumer trends and opportunities for product innovation/enhancements. Determines product features and functions, and oversees product development liaising with cross functional teams. Prepares product documents and supports sales and marketing with the necessary product knowledge and technical expertise. Uses market feedback to evaluate product functionality and drives present/future product direction. Positions are executives leading a major function within local entity/business unit or a part of a regional/global business function. They participate in strategy development as it applies to functional area. Tier 3 Head of Organization usually leads local subsidiaries/divisions. Typically lead directors and managers, but may also lead other Tier 3 executives.

Uses market feedback to evaluate product functionality and drives present/future product direction. A Team Leader (M2) supervises professional level employees (typically entry or experienced level) and may also supervise some para-professional employees. Responsibilities typically include: 'Setting goals and objectives for team members for achievement of operational results. 'Problems faced may be difficult but typically are not complex. 'Ensures policies, practices and procedures are understood and followed by direct reports, customers and stakeholders.

title: Product Commercialization - Executive Tier 2 (ET2)
description: Responsible for the product commercialization process and developing the products from sample stage to production. Provides technical advice to ensure the transition of design into samples and production. Oversees the assigned products from manufacturability to cost and production targets. Partners with the development team to continuously improve the technical applications and production process. Uses commercialization insights to influence internal and external vendors' decisions on price and product value. Positions are top executives typically responsible for a business unit/regional business function. Participate in the development of corporate strategy with specific input to their functional area and line of business. Include corporate function executives reporting to the executive officers of the entire organization. Tier 2 Head of Organization leads a regional business or a local independent company. Typically oversee multiple Tier 3 executives and other Tier 2 executives within their respective function.

title: Trade Marketing - Executive Tier 2 (ET2)
description: Trade Marketing is marketing that targets distributors, wholesalers, and retailers with the goal of increasing opportunities for end-users to purchase products including: 'Offering sales promotions/discounts and incentives to display products 'Providing training on product benefits and uses 'Increasing value for channel partners through improved logistics (i.e., on-time delivery, shorter product lead times, consistent product availability, more accurate invoicing, etc.) 'Increasing end-user satisfaction with the channel partner through high quality products, ease of returns, etc. Positions are top executives typically responsible for a business unit/regional business function. title: Category Management - Executive Tier 2 (ET2)
description: Category Management work is focused on maximizing sales/profitability for a category of consumer products by aligning manufacturer pricing, selection, and promotions for the category with the retailer's role/profit margin for the category including: 'Analyzing and sharing customer/shopper research with retail partners as input to joint category plans and reviews (e.g., category demand projections, consumer shopping trends, purchasing behaviors, price/discount sensitivity, brand/retailer loyalty, etc.) 'Researching the role that the category plays in driving foot traffic, sales volume, and profitability for the retailer (e.g., low price leader for certain items, higher price for others, highest quality or best selection for certain categories, etc.) 'Offering discounts and promotions to support the retailer's role for the category (e.g., aligning manufacturer discounts with retailer low price items, ensuring adequate product supply during promotions, manufacturer coupons for bundling low and high cost items, etc.) in exchange for favorable product stocking and placement vs. competitors 'Monitoring and reviewing manufacturer and retailer performance against category targets Positions are top executives typically responsible for a business unit/regional business function. Participate in the development of corporate strategy with specific input to their functional area and line of business. Include corporate function executives reporting to the executive officers of the entire organization. Tier 2 Head of Organization leads a regional business or a local independent company. Typically oversee multiple Tier 3 executives and other Tier 2 executives within their respective function.

Continuously monitors advertising success. Compiles metrics and analyzes operational performance for the organization's sales efforts, and produces post-campaign reports. Creates and maintains online/digital advertising calendar. An Entry Para-Professional (S1) is an entry-level position typically requiring little to no prior knowledge or experience. Responsibilities typically include: ' Work is routine or follows standard procedures. ' Work is closely supervised. ' Communicates information that requires little explanation or interpretation.

title: Online/Digital Advertising - Experienced Para-Professional (S2)
description: Responsible for online/digital advertising, including but not limited to, display ads, banner ads, news feed ads, and pop-up/under ads via email, the web, social media, mobile devices, etc. Identifies advertising requirements and coordinates the addition of landing pages and banners that target specific brands, product lines, or product types to drive increased search relevancy and create interest in selected products. Continuously monitors advertising success. Compiles metrics and analyzes operational performance for the organization's sales efforts, and produces post-campaign reports. Creates and maintains online/digital advertising calendar. An Experienced Para-Professional (S2) requires basic knowledge of job procedures and tools obtained through work experience and may require vocational or technical education. Responsibilities typically include: 'Works under moderate supervision. 'Problems are typically of a routine nature, but may at times require interpretation or deviation from standard procedures. 'Communicates information that requires some explanation or interpretation.

title: Online/Digital Advertising - Senior Para-Professional (S3)
description: Responsible for online/digital advertising, including but not limited to, display ads, banner ads, news feed ads, and pop-up/under ads via email, the web, social media, mobile devices, etc. Identifies advertising requirements and coordinates the addition of landing pages and banners that target specific brands, product lines, or product types to drive increased search relevancy and create interest in selected products. Continuously monitors advertising success. Compiles metrics and analyzes operational performance for the organization's sales efforts, and produces post-campaign reports. Creates and maintains online/digital advertising calendar. A Senior Para-Professional (S3) requires broad knowledge of operational procedures and tools obtained through extensive work experience and may require vocational or technical education. Responsibilities typically include: ' Works under limited supervision for routine situations. ' Provides assistance and training to lower level employees. ' Problems typically are not routine and require analysis to understand.

title: Market Research - Executive Tier 2 (ET2)
description: Market Research work focuses on gathering, analyzing, and interpreting marketplace trends and customer data as input to marketing and product development strategies/plans for individual consumers, business customers, and/or retail channels including: 'Monitoring market conditions, trends, competitors, etc. to project business sector/consumer demand and inform product launch timing 'Researching competitor behavior including marketing strategies, pricing, sales and methods of operation 'Monitoring media coverage and polling/interviewing customers and/or retailers to determine perceptions of brands/products 'Identifying purchasing behaviors, price/discount sensitivity, brand loyalty, etc. across customer segments and sales channels 'Simulating the impact of proposed marketing campaigns and measuring results against targets Positions are top executives typically responsible for a business unit/regional business function. Participate in the development of corporate strategy with specific input to their functional area and line of business. Include corporate function executives reporting to the executive officers of the entire organization. Tier 2 Head of Organization leads a regional business or a local independent company. Typically oversee multiple Tier 3 executives and other Tier 2 executives within their respective function.

title: Market Research - Executive Tier 3 (ET3)
description: Market Research work focuses on gathering, analyzing, and interpreting marketplace trends and customer data as input to marketing and product development strategies/plans for individual consumers, business customers, and/or retail channels including: 'Monitoring market conditions, trends, competitors, etc. to project business sector/consumer demand and inform product launch timing 'Researching competitor behavior including marketing strategies, pricing, sales and methods of operation 'Monitoring media coverage and polling/interviewing customers and/or retailers to determine perceptions of brands/products 'Identifying purchasing behaviors, price/discount sensitivity, brand loyalty, etc. across customer segments and sales channels 'Simulating the impact of proposed marketing campaigns and measuring results against targets Positions are executives leading a major function within local entity/business unit or a part of a regional/global business function. They participate in strategy development as it applies to functional area. Tier 3 Head of Organization usually leads local subsidiaries/divisions. Typically lead directors and managers, but may also lead other Tier 3 executives.

title: General Field Sales: Chemical - Team Leader (Professionals) (M2)
description: Responsible for selling basic and end chemical products including, but not limited to polymers, intermediates, inorganics, specialty chemicals, industrial gas, agrichemicals, flavors & fragrances, coatings & adhesives. Delivers sales bids/proposals/presentations and conducts product demonstrations. Assesses clients' business needs and offers appropriate recommendations. Acts as an intermediary between customers and organization's departments. Facilitates cross-functional cooperation to translate the overall scientific vision into the final products. Applies advanced skills to provide technical support to customers, escalating as required. Develops and maintains deep understanding of the market structure, competition, existing and potential clients in a given segment to create commercial opportunities. Manages and reviews files, including technical documentation, safety protocols and legal documents covering comprehensive chemical information (such as substance identification, chemical composition, personal protection and accidental release measures, stability and reactivity or physical and chemical properties of the products). Attends seminars, panels and exhibitions within the field of relevant industries to develop professional expertise and stay up-to-date with the latest trends and emerging technologies. A Team Leader (M2) supervises professional level employees (typically entry or experienced level) and may also supervise some para-professional employees. Number of potential clients may be limited and the sales timeframe may be lengthy depending on the IP to be sold. Negotiates the contract for the company to obtain the most profitable deal by estimating how much the client may be willing to pay and estimating potential future earnings from the Intellectual Property. Explains to clients the range of values that may be assessed. Understands legal and intellectual property law principles. Coordinates delivery and transition details, and ensures all IP related standards and processes are followed. An Entry Professional (P1) applies broad theoretical job knowledge typically obtained through advanced education. Responsibilities typically include: ' Work is closely supervised. ' Problems faced are not typically difficult or complex. ' Explains facts, policies and practices related to job area.

title: Direct Sales - Executive Tier 2 (ET2)
description: Direct Sales involves selling directly to the end consumer/business purchaser through primarily field (i.e., face-to-face) interactions with customers including: 'New sales to new customers as well as cross/up/repeat sales to existing customers 'Assessing customer needs and suggesting appropriate products, services, and/or solutions 'Developing and delivering sales bids, proposals, and presentations and conducting product demonstrations Positions are top executives typically responsible for a business unit/regional business function. Participate in the development of corporate strategy with specific input to their functional area and line of business. Include corporate function executives reporting to the executive officers of the entire organization. Tier 2 Head of Organization leads a regional business or a local independent company. Typically oversee multiple Tier 3 executives and other Tier 2 executives within their respective function.

title: Direct Sales - Executive Tier 3 (ET3)
description: Direct Sales involves selling directly to the end consumer/business purchaser through primarily field (i.e., face-to-face) interactions with customers including: 'New sales to new customers as well as cross/up/repeat sales to existing customers 'Assessing customer needs and suggesting appropriate products, services, and/or solutions 'Developing and delivering sales bids, proposals, and presentations and conducting product demonstrations Positions are executives leading a major function within local entity/business unit or a part of a regional/global business function. They participate in strategy development as it applies to functional area. Tier 3 Head of Organization usually leads local subsidiaries/divisions. Typically lead directors and managers, but may also lead other Tier 3 executives.

title: Order Management - Executive Tier 2 (ET2)
description: Responsible for providing order management services to clients. title: Sales Enablement - Executive Tier 2 (ET2)
description: Responsible for developing and executing strategies that enable sales representatives to optimize every buyer interaction in order to build relationships and pipeline, progress opportunities, and close deals. Utilizes knowledge and proficiencies in buyer behaviors, go-to-market process, enablement content, tools and methods, and performance measurement. Assesses information, timing, and context of messaging to help sales representatives engage clients through differentiated experiences to identify, advance and close sales opportunities. Assists sales representatives to achieve targets and objectives by equipping sellers with insights, knowledge, tools, techniques, coaching and facilitating. Positions are top executives typically responsible for a business unit/regional business function. Include corporate function executives reporting to the executive officers of the entire organization. Tier 2 Head of Organization leads a regional business or a local independent company. Typically oversee multiple Tier 3 executives and other Tier 2 executives within their respective function.

title: Live Streaming Sales Operations - Team Leader (Professionals) (M2)
description: 'Manages and operates the technical aspects of multiple live commerce events. 'Sets up and tests the live streaming equipment, software, and network. Troubleshoots any technical issues that may arise during live commerce events. 'Provides customer service and support to the live commerce hosts and viewers. 'Reports and evaluates the results and feedback of the live commerce events. 'Coordinates and delegates work to the live commerce hosts according to the live commerce schedule and objectives. Integrates and maintains synchronization between live streams and e-commerce platforms, ensuring accurate product listings, inventory, and instant purchase capabilities.'
